macro_drivers.label: 2Y Yield
macro_drivers.reading: 3.80, -0.02 from 3.82; 10Y flat at 4.451; bull steepener; Fed held 3.5-3.75% June 17 (Warsh first meeting, dots pushed to 2027-28) — market ignoring hawkish tilt, 2Y barely above upper bound

macro_drivers.label: Net Liquidity (TGA + RRP)
macro_drivers.reading: TGA -$24.6B this week (Treasury spending, cash injected); RRP drained to $0.25B from $6.83B — effectively zero, full depletion; dual liquidity flush into system

macro_drivers.label: COT Crowding
macro_drivers.reading: Nasdaq spec net -3.3%, 2nd percentile — extreme crowded short; entire spec book was war-positioned short for Iran conflict; ceasefire deal catches every short wrong-footed; maximum forced-cover fuel

macro_drivers.label: Iran War / Ceasefire Catalyst
macro_drivers.reading: US-Iran interim MOU signed June 15-17; Strait of Hormuz reopened (12.5M bbl/night per Vance June 20); Vance in Switzerland June 21 finalizing deal details; $6B frozen Qatar assets to be released; Trump threats to 'hit Iran very hard' create deal-collapse tail risk

macro_drivers.label: BOJ / Carry Trade
macro_drivers.reading: BOJ hiked to 1.0% at June meeting; headline flags two more possible hikes; ~$500B yen carry outstanding (Morgan Stanley); USD/JPY 161.23 with mild yen bid (-0.118); systemic unwind risk is week-level, not a Monday catalyst

macro_drivers.label: FOMC / Warsh
macro_drivers.reading: Held unanimously at 3.5-3.75% June 17; dot plot removed 2026 cut, pushed to 2027-28 citing Iran-war inflation spike; Warsh nod to possible future hikes; but 2Y at 3.80 shows market not pricing additional moves

macro_drivers.label: Cross-Asset Overlay
macro_drivers.reading: Gold -$73 (no haven bid — risk-on confirmation); HYG +$0.28 (credit market risk-on); Brent +$0.74 to $80.59 = Trump-threat deal-fragility premium NOT a new supply disruption (Strait is open); VIX +0.38 to 16.78 (low); copper -0.76%, BTC -0.74% (mild growth caution)

move_extent: ~10.2 pts (1.37%) from 739.8 to 750 call wall; above market's own 6.82-pt implied expected move — consistent with short-squeeze regime
completion_signal: 750 tag with call vol/OI ratios collapsing; fresh puts appearing AT 750; negative charm -13,017 at 750 triggers dealer delta-selling as 0DTE calls decay; price chops/stalls above 750 on first touch
manipulation_tell: Massive put concentration at 738 (1049% vol/OI) and 739 (1006%) right at spot may script an opening stop-flush sweep to gamma flip 737.5; call buying INTO that sub-738 dip is the real_pump confirmation — do not short the flush

reversal_zones.note: Primary call wall; GEX 238.5M + charm -13,017; board 63% reversal prob; main stall and fade zone — expect heavy put buying and call selling at first touch
Board 42% reversal prob; large negative charm cluster -9,113; interim test on the run to 750 — consolidation zone if squeeze loses steam early
Board 55% reversal prob; positive charm +3,759 at 733; fresh call flow 150% vol/OI; strong dealer DEX buy zone on any intraday dip — highest-probability bounce below spot
Board 40% reversal prob; GEX -126.9M (large negative = dealers buy aggressively); downside anchor if 732 fails under a Trump/Iran escalation headline shock

summary: Macro gravity is firmly bullish into Monday June 22: the 2-year yield eased 2bp to 3.80 (10-year flat at 4.451, clean bull steepener), the TGA shed $24.6B this week injecting cash while the RRP drained to near-zero at $0.25B — full liquidity depletion — and NASDAQ speculative positioning sits at the 2nd COT percentile, the most crowded short read in years; critically, that short book was positioned for the ongoing Iran war, and the June 15-17 interim MOU (Strait reopened, Iran selling oil again, $6B frozen assets releasing) catches every war-short wrong-footed at once — Vance is in Switzerland right now working on deal details, which is the forced-cover catalyst. Monday has zero scheduled data releases (PCE not until Thursday June 25), making it a clean tape day for flow mechanics to dominate. The options structure confirms real_pump: spot 739.8 is 2.3 pts above the gamma flip at 737.5, DEX is strongly positive at +7,552 (dealers buy every dip), GEX is positive (cushions drops), and fresh call flow spans 31 strikes from 731 to 758 with monster vol/OI ratios concentrated at 737.5 (858%), 741 (468%), 737 (489%), and 738 (469%) — too broad and too coordinated for one trader, this is institutional squeeze positioning. ATM IV of 14.54 and VIX at 16.78 (low, slight uptick) point to a clean trending day. True expansion is up to 750 (board 63% reversal, GEX 238.5M, charm -13,017 — the primary stall and fade zone) from 739.8 = 10.2 pts (1.37%), above the 6.82-pt implied expected move, with 745 (42% reversal, charm -9,113) as the interim resistance test; above 750 the next cluster is 755. Opening tell: the enormous put concentration at 738 (1049% vol/OI) and 739 (1006%) right at spot may engineer a brief stop-flush sweep to the 737.5 gamma flip at the open — call buying into that sub-738 dip is the real_pump confirmation signal and the key tell for the day. Reversal support below: 732 (board 55%, positive charm, strong fresh call flow) then 728 (40%, GEX -126.9M = hard dealer buy). Cross-asset confirms risk-on: gold is falling $73 (no haven bid), HYG is firming, and the Brent rise to $80.59 (+$0.74) is Trump-threat deal-fragility premium — NOT a new supply disruption (the Strait is physically open) — so if Switzerland talks deliver any progress today, oil fades and the squeeze accelerates. Primary tail risks: Trump escalates publicly and deal collapses (Strait re-closes, oil spikes above $85, fast reversal from 750); BOJ double-hike risk (USD/JPY 161.23) is the week-level systemic overhang but has no Monday meeting catalyst.

Trump threatens to 'hit Iran very hard again' while Vance negotiates; Iran negotiating team protests, cites MOU violation — deal fragility risk
Fed held 3.5-3.75% June 17 (Warsh debut); dot plot erased 2026 cuts; next reductions 2027-28 on Iran-war inflation spike (PCE est. +4.1% YoY due Thursday June 25)
Monday June 22 carries zero major US economic data releases — clean tape day for flow to dominate
OPEC cut 2026 demand growth to 970,000 bpd; May production 190,000 bpd below April partly from Strait closure disruptions — supply picture still adjusting to reopening
